_meta.title: Coffee Can Investing — Quantitative Specification Ledger
_meta.author: Saurabh Mukherjea, Rakshit Ranjan, Pranab Uniyal (Penguin, 2018)
_meta.purpose: Authoritative source of truth for all quantitative thresholds in the Coffee Can Twin Filters and Clean Accounts engine. Pairs symmetrically with ub_financial_specs.json to prevent structural code drift.
_meta.audit_note: All source_quote entries are verbatim from the book. Gemini-generated spec in bugs.txt contained a fabricated quote for the CFO/EBITDA threshold ('Below 80 per cent is an immediate red flag') that does NOT appear in the book. No explicit percentage threshold is given in the book for CFO/Cumulative EBITDA — the 90% gate is our engineering proxy, documented below.

twin_filters_core._source_quote_primary: grown sales each year by at least 10 per cent alongside generating Return on Capital Employed (pre-tax) of at least 15 per cent
twin_filters_core._source_quote_appendix: Revenue growth of 10 per cent and ROCE of 15 per cent every year for non-financial services companies

non_financial_companies._note_5y_buffer: 5Y CAGR floor of 8% (not 10%) absorbs macro cycle anomalies (COVID-19). Engineering judgment, not book-stated.
non_financial_companies._note_individual_years: Book requires 10% every single year. Floor relaxed from 10% → 5% to absorb the COVID-19 pandemic anomaly year (FY21 revenue contraction was universal and not a signal of structural failure). CSV provides Revenue 2-5 Years Back individually; years 6-10 covered by 10Y CAGR.

financial_companies_bfsi._source_quote: ROE of 15 per cent: We prefer Return on Equity over Return on Assets because this is a fairer measure of banks' (and other lenders') ability to generate higher income efficiently on a given equity capital base over time
financial_companies_bfsi._source_quote_loan: Loan growth of 15 per cent: Given that nominal GDP growth in India has averaged 13.8 per cent over the past ten years, loan growth of at least 15 per cent is an indication of a bank's ability to lend over business cycles
financial_companies_bfsi._note_proxy: Loan book data not in StockScan CSV. Revenue CAGR used as proxy for credit expansion. ROE routing is exact per book; loan growth proxy is a known limitation documented in implementation_mapping.

cost_of_capital_derivation._source_quote: Adding the risk-free rate (8 per cent in India) to the equity risk premium of 6.5 to 7 per cent gives a cost of capital broadly in that range

clean_accounts_firewall._critical_finding: The book lists CFO/EBITDA as ONE of 11 ratios in a decile-based RELATIVE ranking system. NO explicit percentage threshold (e.g. 90%, 80%) is stated in the book for this ratio. The 90% gate in fw_coffee_can is our engineering proxy — a hard binary threshold derived from industry practice, not a direct book citation.
clean_accounts_firewall._gemini_fabricated_quote_warning: Gemini's bugs.txt claimed the book states 'Below 80 per cent is an immediate red flag.' This quote does NOT appear anywhere in complete_book_parsed.txt. It is fabricated.
ambit_clean_accounts_11_ratios: Volatility in non-operating income (% of net revenues)
Provisioning for doubtful debts as proportion of debtors > 6 months
Change in reserves (excl. share premium) to net income excl. dividends
Contingent liability as proportion of net worth
Miscellaneous expenses as proportion of total revenue
Cumulative CFO plus CFI to median revenues
CAGR in auditors remuneration to CAGR in consolidated revenues

engineering_proxy_used_in_code.rationale: CFO/EBITDA ≥ 90% is the most direct observable proxy for the first of Ambit's 11 ratios (Cumulative CFO / Cumulative EBITDA). The 90% floor ensures that operating cash flow is not being eroded by P&L manipulation. This is a point-in-time approximation of the book's cumulative multi-year ratio.
engineering_proxy_used_in_code.book_basis: Proxy derivation, not a verbatim book threshold. No explicit percentage is given in the book.

leverage_governance_gates._source: Chapter 2 + Appendix 1 (operational filters for portfolio construction)

debt_ceiling_non_financial._note: D/E < 1.0 prevents leverage-inflated ROE from masquerading as genuine capital efficiency. Book identifies high D/E as a disqualifier for ROCE-based companies.

promoter_pledge_ceiling._note: Pledge below 10% as governance pre-filter. Book emphasizes promoter skin-in-game. Pledge > 10% requires investigation; > 20% is a disqualifier.

capital_efficiency_routing.book_requirement: ROCE for non-financials; ROE for BFSI companies

roe_financial_recent.note: Corrected from 12% to 15% — book is explicit: 'ROE of 15 per cent'

revenue_5y_cagr.note: 5Y floor uses 8% (not 10%) to absorb COVID-19 disruption. Engineering judgment.

discrete_annual_velocity.book_requirement: Revenue growth of 10% every single year for 10 consecutive years
discrete_annual_velocity.data_source: data_engine.py COLUMN_MAP: Revenue 2-5 Years Back → revenue_2yb..revenue_5yb
discrete_annual_velocity.fillna_strategy: fillna(5) — missing year treated as meeting threshold (conservative: don't penalize short history)
discrete_annual_velocity.threshold_relaxation_note: Floor relaxed from book's 10% to 5% (pandemic adjustment). COVID-19 FY21 caused universal revenue disruption — penalizing structurally sound businesses for a macro event contradicts the book's intent.

clean_accounts_proxy.scale_note: cfo_to_ebitda stored as percentage (e.g. 92.4 = 92.4%). Threshold 90.0, NOT 0.9

sector_pct_rank_infinity_guard.bug_fixed: np.inf values from near-zero denominators ranked as universe maximum without guard

vectorization.requirement: Zero iterrows(), zero apply() in hot paths

known_gaps_and_mitigations.gap: Loan book growth not available in StockScan CSV — revenue CAGR used as proxy for financials
known_gaps_and_mitigations.impact: Banks with stable revenues but declining loan books could pass the financial sector gate
known_gaps_and_mitigations.mitigation: ROE routing (book's other financial criterion) still provides the primary quality gate

known_gaps_and_mitigations.gap: Individual year revenue checks only cover years 2-5 (CSV provides Revenue 2-5YB)
known_gaps_and_mitigations.impact: Years 6-10 verified only by 10Y CAGR average — a company with one bad year in years 7-9 could pass
known_gaps_and_mitigations.mitigation: 10Y CAGR ≥ 10% + 5Y CAGR ≥ 8% + YoY ≥ 0% together create overlapping coverage

known_gaps_and_mitigations.gap: CFO/EBITDA is a point-in-time ratio; book uses cumulative multi-year ratio
known_gaps_and_mitigations.impact: A single exceptional year can pass the 90% gate without sustained clean accounting
known_gaps_and_mitigations.mitigation: forensic_engine's 21 red flags provide complementary multi-year accounting quality coverage
